seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Instructor enables structured extraction of code suggestions, fixes, and explanations from LLMs, ensuring reliable Pydantic-validated outputs. Guardrails are essential to verify code correctness, prevent security vulnerabilities, and block credential leakage in repository-aware agents. Vex integration catches common Instructor failures like schema validation retries and hallucinated enum values while enforcing coding safety standards.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant follows a code-aware agent loop: repository context is fed into an Instructor-patched LLM client to extract structured CodeSuggestion objects containing fixes, tests, and explanations. The sandbox executes proposed code with test validation, triggering iterative debugging cycles when validation fails. Vex verifies both Instructor outputs and sandbox results against code correctness, security, and compliance policies.

Instructor wraps the LLM client to enforce Pydantic schemas for consistent extraction of code blocks, file paths, and test cases. The agent maintains conversation state across iterations, with Vex providing observability into validation failures and security risks. This creates a reliable feedback loop for production-grade code generation.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: Instructor-extracted code contains shell commands, eval(), or filesystem writes exploiting sandbox escapes
risks.mitigation: Vex static analysis + sandbox permission verification

risks.name: Credential leakage in comments
risks.description: LLM hallucinates API keys or tokens in code comments/examples during fix generation
risks.mitigation: Vex secret scanning on Instructor CodeSuggestion objects

risks.name: Insecure dependency recommendation
risks.description: Assistant suggests compromised or malicious PyPI/npm packages

risks.name: License-incompatible code
risks.description: Extracted code mixes GPL with proprietary code violating compliance
risks.mitigation: Vex license header scanning and compatibility checks

implementation.title: Instructor Pydantic Model for Code Suggestions
implementation.description: Define structured output schema that Instructor validates from LLM responses
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List
import openai

class TestCase(BaseModel):
    input: str
    expected_output: str

class CodeSuggestion(BaseModel):
    file_path: str = Field(..., description="Target file path")
    code_block: str = Field(..., description="Complete code replacement")
    test_cases: List[TestCase] = Field(default=[], description="Validation tests")
    explanation: str = Field(..., description="Why this fix works")

client = from_openai(openai.OpenAI())

implementation.title: Sandbox Test Execution
implementation.description: Execute and validate Instructor-extracted code in isolated environment
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def validate_suggestion(suggestion: CodeSuggestion) -> bool:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        filepath = os.path.join(tmpdir, suggestion.file_path)
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath), exist_ok=True)
        
        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
            f.write(suggestion.code_block)
        
        for test in suggestion.test_cases:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ['python', '-c', f'exec(open("{filepath}").read()); print(buggy_function("{test.input}"))'],
                capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=tmpdir
            )
            if result.stdout.strip() != test.expected_output:
                return False
    return True
